name: Freddie Green
abstract: Frederick William Green (March 31, 1911 – March 1, 1987) was an American swing jazz guitarist who played rhythm guitar with the Count Basie Orchestra for almost fifty years.

concerts.label: Benny Goodman and His Swing Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Newport Jazz Festival: Count Basie and His Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: A Salute to Count Basie

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Freddie Green and I, we were walking someplace in Mississippi... The rhythm section is the backbone to any band... Freddie Green was really the backbone to the rhythm section... Jo Jones and I would say, Freddie Green and Walter Page were the backbone of the rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: Freddie Green was pretty special. I mean you’ve giving a hundred percent all the time, I mean never solo. And why do you think Freddie Green never soloed, because his strings were up so damn — pardon me — so high.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Harry "Sweets" Edison
about_quotes.evidence: And four rhythm section. So I should have paid him to be in the band because I was having so much fun. ... And without Freddie Green, the rhythm section really wouldn’t sound as full as it was with Freddie Green
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry "Sweets" Edison interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: On bass was a gentleman by the name of Gene Ramey I think, either Gene Ramey or Al Hall. My memory is hazy there. And of course Freddie Green, Count Basie, and Gus Johnson was the drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: The group started out with Basie, Gus Johnson on drums, Jimmie Lewis... and Freddie Green, of course, guitar. In the front line was me...
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: But when I first started with the Basie band it was John Duke, myself, and of course Freddie Green, Count Basie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]
